learning.learning_goal: Understand that 'how many fewer' asks for the same gap from the other direction.
learning.big_idea: Fewer compares two amounts by finding the gap.

screens.label: Lesson 002
screens.title: How many fewer?
screens.body: Fewer is another way to compare two groups.

visual.message: Both rows start in the same place. Which row stops sooner?

screens.label: Discover
screens.title: What does fewer ask us to find?

visual.message: The gap shows how many fewer blocks the bottom row has.

choices.feedback: Fewer asks for the gap between the two groups.

hints: The gap tells how many fewer.

screens.label: Language
screens.title: Fewer, less, difference.

visual.message: These words compare the two groups.

screens.guidance: Fewer does not mean guess smaller. It asks how far apart the groups are.

screens.label: Symbol
screens.title: Which equation measures fewer?

visual.message: The gap is still measured from larger to smaller.

choices.feedback: The larger amount minus the smaller amount gives the gap.

hints: Measure the gap from larger to smaller.

visual.message: The bottom row has 6 fewer blocks.

screens.label: Reflect
screens.title: What did you discover?
screens.prompt: Choose the statement that explains the idea.

choices.text: Fewer always means the smaller number is the answer.
Fewer can ask for the gap between two groups.

hints: Think about what the missing part showed.

screens.title: You compared with fewer.
screens.body: You used the same gap idea from a new direction.
